evals.name: python-1to1-verification
evals.prompt: I need to build a Python backend that verifies a user's identity by comparing their selfie against their ID card photo. Use the AIMS API.
evals.expected_output: Complete Python code with: HMAC-SHA256 auth helper, liveness detection call, two detect calls (ID + selfie), verify call, similarity threshold check, error handling for all API responses.
expectations: Code includes HMAC-SHA256 signing with client_secret
Auth token is obtained before other API calls
Liveness detection is called before face verification
Two separate detect calls are made (one for ID card, one for selfie)
Verify endpoint is called with both face_tokens
Similarity threshold of 0.7 is used or mentioned
Error handling covers at least code 401, 461, and 440
client_secret is not hardcoded in the example

evals.name: nodejs-1toN-identification
evals.prompt: Help me create a Node.js Express API for an office access control system. I need to register employee faces and then identify visitors. Use Authme AIMS API.
evals.expected_output: Node.js Express app with: auth module, FaceSet creation endpoint, employee registration endpoint, visitor identification endpoint, proper error handling.
expectations: Express routes are defined for registration and identification
FaceSet is created with display_name (outer_id is optional)
Registration flow: detect → register to FaceSet with external_id
Identification flow: detect → identify with faceset_token and top_n
Auth token caching is implemented (not fetched per-request)
Error responses use proper HTTP status codes
Liveness detection is included or mentioned for the identification flow

evals.name: go-liveness-check
evals.prompt: Write a Go function that checks if an uploaded image contains a real person (not a photo of a photo) using AIMS liveness API.
evals.expected_output: Go function with AIMS auth and liveness detection, returning boolean result with score.
expectations: Go code uses crypto/hmac and crypto/sha256 for signing
Multipart form data is correctly constructed for image upload
Authorization Bearer header is set
Liveness score threshold of 0.5 is checked
Response JSON is properly parsed
Error handling for code 404 (no face) and 503 (service unavailable)

evals.name: python-ocr-with-quality-gate
evals.prompt: Help me integrate the AIMS OCR API in Python to read a Taiwan ID card. Run the quality check first, then OCR, and produce user-facing guidance for each quality failure. The client retries on transient errors.
evals.expected_output: Python code with: token caching, quality check call, OCR call with autoRotate, Idempotency-Key for OCR retries, mapped user-facing messages for QC status codes 457-468, and error handling.
expectations: Calls POST /aims/ocr/qualitycheck before POST /aims/ocr/image
Quality check request includes both image and cardType=TWN_IDCard_Front
OCR request includes autoRotate=true (or explains why omitted)
OCR call sets Idempotency-Key header using a UUID
Same Idempotency-Key is reused across retries of the same logical operation (not regenerated per HTTP attempt)
User-facing messages are produced for at least 3 of the 12 QC status codes (457-468)
Error handling distinguishes 400/404/422/429/503 from QC failures
client_secret is not hardcoded
